pages.sketch_desc: Outline map of France with a route line drawn from le thore / Vallée de la Seine in the north, down through Châteaux de la Loire and Bourges, then south-west to Périgord Caves, and south to Gorges de Truyères, Cassaniouze, and Conques. Year label '1992' appears to the right of the map.
pages.text:
We drove along winding roads through beautiful little villages, & arrived at Etraques, where it's market day, to shop. Then on up the valley of the Truyères, with hydro electric dams, & purpose-built 1930's villages.

[Route sketch of France, labelled 1992, with route line showing:] le thore / Vallée de la Seine / Châteaux de la Loire / Bourges / Périgord Caves / Gorges de Truyères / Cassaniouze / Conques

We had lunch in a very dirty wood & came on to a crowded, but pleasant campsite on the La Source* * * Lac du Sarrans, created by the Sarrans

pages.sketch_desc: Hand-drawn diagram of the swimming pool layout at the campsite, showing: chute on the left leading into the pool, 'deep' and 'swimming' sections, a bridge above, sun-warmed seat at top left, foot bath at top right, a 'shoe' area, '4 sprays into pool' entering from the right, 'steps down', and a 'chil's pool+slide' at bottom left. Also annotated 'eating' area outside the pool. Caption reads 'Pool in confined area at campsite.'
pages.text:
damn. There's a swimming pool where we all enjoyed swimming, & John grew more & more confident swimming out from the chute. We all had an ice cream afterwards to celebrate his swimming the width.

[Sketch of pool layout at campsite, labelled:] sun-warmed seat / bridge / foot bath / chute / deep / swimming / shoe / 4 sprays into pool / steps down / chil's pool+slide

Pool in confined area at campsite.     eating

Wash-rooms under tennis court.

__Wed August 5th__

      We wanted to leave early, but stayed for a swim. The pool only opened at 10.00.. We drove north [past] out of the Gorges & the Chestnut Trees. We spent hours on the A71, going past villages, castles & farm houses. We drove for 8 hours & ended up camping in quite a pretty campsite in Salbris * * *. The restaurant,

Hotel du Parc, was the best, most friendly of the holiday. I had pâté, fish (lieu-noir?) fromage-frais with chives + chocolate mousse pudding. The sign on paying in the lobby stated how much it was per personne, deux personne + chien R.C. 50fr. Anglian dogs obviously don't cross the channel.

__Thu August 6th__
		We chatted to a couple from London in the next tent who had been to the Olympics in Barcelona. They complained that ticket purchasing was not centralised.
		We set off towards Chartres + arrived at 12.50. Thomas + Eve spent an hour looking at stained-glass windows starting to devise a quiz for the B'ham cathedral choir trip in October.
		We then paid £13 for a snack lunch in a bar - jambon et fromage croissants, icecream + coffee.

After another 1½ hours' motoring we reached __St. Illiers la Ville__ ****+
jumped into the swimming pool which is perhaps rather heavily chlorinated.
(See P.80.).
		There is also a large trampoline which Peter particularly enjoys. Ten or so children sit around the edge of it, while each child in turn has a bounce in the middle, or else 5 or so children bounce up + down all at once. A P.E. instructor's nightmare. The trampoline is in a wood under tall trees in the shade, along with 2 ping pong tables.
		The site is only 5 minutes from the station, + the train run into Paris is 50 minutes, so it would be possible to do day trips to Paris or ~~De~~ Eurodisney from the site.
__Fri 6th August__
		We lazed about at the campsite enjoying the trampoline + swimming,

had lunch, + then set off to shop in Brownie + at a supermarket. We arrived at the campsite at 5.30. Laura + her father + Benjamin had already been here an hour, because we had said that we would arrive at 4. It is peaceful with an orchard + ping pong. Quite low key + old showers, but clean + friendly.
	Eve beat everyone at ping pong, except Thomas, who beat Eve 22-20.

__Sat 7th Aug__
	We got up at 5.3[0]15. + left at 6.15, without breakfast. We ate breakfast while mincing along in the traffic jams. On the boat we just had sandwiches + watched Sean Connery in "Medicine Man".
	After Southampton we called in on Hathie, but unfortunately they were all out. There were awful traffic jams. Finally we stopped + had tea in an almost

deserted Newbury station, where we picked hundreds of blackberries. After Newbury the traffic moved faster up to Birmingham.
	It was a fabulous holiday. Noone got sick. Thomas, William + Peter now have a rota for doing the washing up + drying up, while Eve reads aloud, which makes life much easier.
	Thomas most liked Cassanouse; William most liked the pedal boats + pedalo fights, + Peter most liked the first French restaurant we went to, St. Pierre, where we ate just what they put in front of us, + we did not choose from a menu.

__Sat 15th August__
	We set off at 10.35., Eve, Laura, Thomas, William, Peter, John + Charles, to spend the night at Annette's on the

way to Thorpeness. We had lunch in a field; Eve had bought tip-over yoghurts, & most of the children tipped the yoghurt over themselves while tipping the flavour into the yoghurt.
	At Annette's Eve made tea, & then we went for a walk with Penel & the 2 dogs, Lucy & Pippa. Very energetic, romping puppies. We met Charles Henneker on the way. His son, Penel's godson, Thomas, 9, was spending the night with Penel. Thomas slept with Penel & Thomas, while Laura, William & Peter slept in the house & John & Charles & Eve were in the van. It rained in the night, but was clear next day. It was quite a squash getting up onto the flat bit of Annette's drive. Thomas & Annette had to hold the tree branches back. Annette cooked us a very good barbeque – she had started the charcoal

in a jug.

__Sunday 16th August__
	Annette gave us breakfast & we went to church at Eye. Lovely high church. We had to return to Thornham Magna because Thomas had forgotten his cap.
	We reached __Thorpeness__ in time for lunch.
	Jane feeds us lunch & we have camping van breakfasts & suppers every day. I hope it doesn't rain.

__Sunday 24th August__
	__Friday__ Yesterday Simon joined us. Today he drove off at 10·15. to take Thomas to Beauchamp. Then Simon drove home to B'ham. Everyone else went home in the rela[illegible]

__Friday 29th August__
	Simon, Eve, [strikethrough: Laura], William, Peter, John, Charles went down for the Beauchamp

concert, William babysat John + Charles in the van while Simon, Eve, William + Peter went in to hear Thomas play in a Mozart quartet.
__Saturday 30th August__
	We went into Gloucester + looked around the antique warehouse + the walked right around the old docks at Gloucester, looking at the history + layout. After Thomas' orchestral concert (works by Thea Musgrave, Schubert's Unfinished Symphony, Barber of Seville by Rossini, Britten's Matinée Musicale), we drove home

__Saturday 5th September__
	We set off at 8.50. + had breakfast on the motorway services. Not a good idea — queues + queue-bargers. We arrived at the __Peak District__ + walked for 3 hours in __Goyt Valley__. Good weather, despite the forecast. People paragliding near a disused railway.
	At 3.30 we left for Buxton + walk[ed]

into the town. We saw the museum + art gallery, with models of prehistoric scenes. The Crescent is boarded up. The proportions are lovely, like Bath, but not so large + awe-inspiring. There is also a hospital with a dome + a hotel in the same style. The Pavillion, glassed-in, reminded us of Malvern. We saw the spring where the water is bottled.
	We reached the campsite at 6.0. King Sterndale, east of Buxton.
	We camped across the road from an old church which only had a service in the afternoon. The evening was fine, but it started to rain in the night. Siri, the new German au pair, moved into Thomas's + Peter's tent in the middle of the night, + Thomas obligingly moved out into hers.

On the Sunday Simon, Thomas, Eve & John attempted a walk, but the rain was coming across horizontally, so we gave up…. & all went home. Sir left 5 days later.

__Friday October 23rd. 1992__
   Simon, Eve, William, John & Charles set out for York. (Thomas & Peter singing with Birmingham Cathedral choir in Paris & Chartres.) We leave at 2.10. It's raining + there are dreadful, sudden jams on the M1 between Nottingham & Sheffield. We overshoot the turning off to York because it is not marked. Finally we reach __York__ almost via Wetherby.
   We end up on the __Rowntree Park__ Caravan Site, near the river just south

of the city.

__Saturday__
   We walk into York along the river past where the boat left us 3 years ago. We reach the __Jorvic Centre__ at 9.25., just before the crowd starts. It's a fabulous museum, & fully lived up to my expectations, I had long wanted to visit it. It's important to spend the night in York & get there early. We bought a big tapestry in the museum shop for us all to do.
   We had coffee, hot chocolate & croissants & then went on to the __Minster__. Soaring, simple, disciplined architecture, & a myriad of things to look at below. A Miner in a little hole in the wall (model) ent[illegible]

A roof boss of a diver + whale + between them the bubbles from the aqualung. The epitaphs included a description of a man whose.
   "Witticisms gild the joyful hour
   And kindness lasts when the hour of joy
      is o'er"

   After the Minster we saw the __Treasurer's House__, restored after 1900 by Frank Green. Green was the grandson of a man who invented the "Economiser." The "economiser" used left-over heat from boilers to pre-heat the water. Frank Green put the family's money to good use in the house. It's small, liveable in + well worth a visit.
   We had a pizza lunch + set off for the __National Railway__

__Museum__.
   We looked at the Mail coaches with nets, the Royal Coaches, how rails used to be laid + the section of the steam train.
   We walked home along the __city walls__. It was almost raining. Beautiful views of distance + sky + a rainbow. The houses as seen from the walls were interesting to compare with Richlieu.
   John + Charles walked amazingly well, about 4 miles.
   We had supper + Eve read "Lord of the Rings" while William did tapestry.
   Rain came on in the night.

__Sunday October 25th__
      It rained + rained. The Clocks changed + we packed up in a leisurely way + parked outside the city walls next to St. John's Cof E college. We went to a service at the Minster. The volume of singing from the choir seemed rather thin. Donald English preached a "whoaa! look at dat!" sermon on the Gospel reading "In the Beginning was the word." There was a good sound system with a funky twiddling knobs near us at the back. Getting to the loo was a major performance for Charles.
delicious We set off south + had mountains of Yorkshire pudding + very dead roast beef at "The Fox" near Pontefract.
      In the afternoon we stopped to look at Conisborough Castle. Even in the rain + north wind it is very impressive, a white circular keep. Sir

Walter Scott mentions it in "Ivanhoe." There was an information room where John + Charles tried on Medieval clothes, tunics + hats.
      The keep had fireplaces + latrines + a chapel + vestry. The Chapel had carved stone, rare work in a cast[le]

Su[?]

      The bailey has the stone outlines of various castle buildings, kitchens, stable, chapel, all in the green grass.
[photo: family group of adults and children eating at a folding table outside a white camper van on a grassy campsite, trees in background]

***** campsite good enough to plan a holiday aroun[d]
**** campsite worth going out of our way to visit
*** good campsite
** Not special but fairly good site

-x below average not really worth visiting
-XX only go to if no others are available
-XXX Bad site
-XXXX A campsite to go out of your way not to visit
-XXXXX A despicable site to avoid at all costs.

places: Sierra de la Peña de Francia
pages.sketch_desc: Hand-drawn outline map of Spain and Portugal with a route marked in red (and some blue) lines. Labelled points A–E mark altitude stops: (A) Picos de Europa near León in the north; (B) unlabelled on map but referenced in key as Cebreiro, in the northwest; (C) Sierra area on right/east side; (D) Montemuro/El Portillo area; (E) marked near Bilbao in the north with filled circle. Santiago de Compostela is labelled in lower-left of map; Braga and Oporto are in Portugal (lower centre); Salamanca is on the right; 'Sierra de la Peña de Francia' is labelled on the right side. Arrows along the top of the map suggest mountain passes or direction of travel. The route appears to loop around northern and central Spain and into Portugal.
pages.text:
Katrien Spain
p. 67 - end.

[Hand-drawn route map of Spain and Portugal with the following place labels on the map: Santiago, Bilbao, Picos de Europa (A), León, Salamanca, Santiago de Compostela, Braga, Oporto, Sierra de la Peña de Francia (C/D area)]

passin Picos 1250 m. (A)
[?after] Cebreiro 1337 (B)
Sierra de 1200 (C)
Montemuro 1240 (D)
El Portillo
Las Huertas 1710 (E)
Santa [?] Ines

April 16th 1993 Friday.

We set off from home at 2.40. on Charles' birthday.
    Simon 43, [J]Eve 39, Thomas 13, William 11, Peter 9, John 5, Charles 4.
    It is the last weekend of the Easter holidays.
    We stopped in a lay-by for Charle[s'] chocolate birthday cake.
    The Ogbourne St. George ****
was very pretty, big open chalk fields, hills, at a distance, all roun[d]
Only one chemical toilet, John got out & ran up the hill. Thomas & Pete[r] slept in the tent. John started off in the [to]p bunk & then moved to the bottom. [I]ve got a metal splinter from the torch in the middle of the night. Maglite

April 17th.
	We visited Di + Ian. She opened the door with "Hello! Would you like a drink + a pee?" We had elevenses + drove on to

[sketch of David + Maxine's House, Newbury, labelled: kitchen, eating room]

David + Maxine's House, Newbury.
[?Bley Lodge]

Hungerford. We wandered around antique shops, Simon looking for dining room chairs (unsuccessful) + Eve looking for hair pins (successful), and on for lunch + the night to David + Maxines + Imogen 3½ + Iona 2 at Newbury.
	We went on a long walk past "The & 5 Bells, very pretty, to the Watermill Theatre – lovely auditorium, + back for tea.
	Bgoats in the field, kite flying.
	Thomas + William slept at the bottom of the garden.
	Imogen, Iona, John + Charles rolled down the hill in the caterpillar + the little [illegible].

April 18th
	We had breakfast – gorgeous fry-up in the house, + left for Hattie + James at Chandlers Ford. We had a delicious roast lunch, + chatted. James caught a bus to Heathrow, plane to Rome + then Cairo, then minibus!

Alexandra, working for Cunard personel.
Anna came back from her job as a chamber-
maid. Katrina was doing community service at an
old peoples' home.
	Simon drove all of us + Hattie +
Katrina, Laura + Edward to see __Winchester__
__Cathedral__. It was lovely to see all the
Dugoids. Simon + Eve slept in the new extension.

	__April 19th__
	Hattie left for work on her first day
of term, having calmly given everyone breakfast.
Then we set off for __Romsey Rapids__.
	The Rapids £11-20 for 1 adult,
5 children, were great fun. We had
hot chocolate afterwards.
	We drove to __Portsmouth__ + ate in the
carpark, surrounded by hoardes of French
students.
	The HMS Victory, Nelson's flagship
was fascinating. The guide pretended Peter
[?to] [illegible] at 10 miles per hour

captions: Bell tower Portsmouth carpark.  St Anne's Chu[rch]
pages.text:
Bell tower Portsmouth carpark.  St Anne's Chu[rch]

[Hand-drawn sketch of a bell tower / cupola with weather vane (showing S and N compass points) above a domed roof supported by columns, sitting atop a square tower base, with a flat rooftop in the foreground]

was the "powder monkey", sitting on a biscuit barrel, responsible for the 14 men in his group. 8 pints beer once they were 14. 500 of the 800 men slept on one deck. 60% press ganged. No room to swing a cat.
	Then we looked at the Mary Rose being sprayed, a wax which will impregnate the wood. She should have had only 400 men, but 700 were on her the day she sank. Sir George Carew couldn't control them.
	Ropes went out the stern of the ship from a reel, showing how many "knots" the speed went.
	Shoes, clothes, medical equipment, cooking, arrows, bows, guns.

captions: Angels carved in bone, found in a chest on the main deck, may have formed part of the spine of a book.  Mary Rose
pages.text: Angels carved in bone, found in a chest on the main deck, may have formed part of the spine of a book.		Mary Rose

We drove on into Surrey & camped near Leatherhead. Horsley *** the site was very wet, boggy grass & a lake, but had a shop & was friendly. Supper, lovely in Indian restaurant.
__Tues Apl 20th__
   We set off into a Danish shop in Leatherhead & Simon rescued his lights. Meanwhile Thomas & Eve were doing battle with a fractious John & Charles & exceedingly irate shop-owner to get sandwiches for lunch.
   We drove on to London & Thomas found money for the metre for the van parked in __Finsbury Circus__, while Simon tried to mend his lamps on a building being renovated. William got everyone into Finsbury Circus Park for a picnic. (The oldest park in the city - the "fen where water went under the Roman wall - made into a park in 1600s.)
   We had a tiring, [?ighty] drive home.

Saturday 8th May
   An exhilarating day! We left at 8.25 am, & had breakfast on the way. We reached the __Manifold Valley__ & rented bikes & had a 3 hour bike ride with a picnic lunch. Charles rode on the back of Simon's bike. John had only learnt to ride a 2-wheeler a month before, & found it difficult at first, but by the end he was cycling very skilfully & leading the way. 12[illegible] mi[illegible]
   We are camped in a lovely sheltered field near Broughton Hall, a Franciscan convent which is about to be sold. Fair Oak *** campsite
   Thomas has helped Simon to park the van, William set off with John & Charles & his camera to photograph a stream "runn[illegible] water" & Peter is flying his kite. Eve has played Boules with John & Charles.
   Charles went to a farm party yesterday & saw sheep & lambs & cows & "bullets," much to William's amusement, who keeps asking him how fast they go.
   We tottered over to the pub, the Freemason's Arms, for a drink before supper.

Sunday May 9th.
      We are camped east of Market Drayton, near a village called "Loggerheads". It's a very good campsite in a sheltered valley. The pub is about to build a play area.
      It rained heavily in the night. At 10 we set off for church, a prayer book Communion at 11 in the old church which, they said, had been the chapel of Broughton Hall. It was very, very friendly. One girl + one baby among all the old people in the congregation, and an old German vicar. The boys, Thomas + Peter, returned indignantly from the altar rail complaining that they had not been blessed - in fact they had not waited for the vicar who was offering the chalice.
      It was an unchanged church - amazing with old boxed pews. Lovely. The boys sat in one + squabbled. Simon + Charlotte + I sat behind. Charles waited for his goo[?rag]. The plan of the boys' box was [sketch of rectangular box with arrow] cosy warm.
      + had seats on 3 sides.

Afterwards we set off on a 4½ mile walk, everything bursting green. It was quite windy. For our picnic we had a choice of wind or flies in the woods. We ended up managing to sit between. It was a lovely walk over stiles, through woods + pastures, past farms, houses built on lakes. Red sandstone. At the end we came down through a steep-sided green valley, very narrow.
      Peter liked the walk more than the bike ride because there were fewer people. Indeed, we only met one couple. William + I liked the bike ride, but I am glad that there weren't more people than there were.
      Stopping at the Wolverhampton camping shop on the way home, Peter bought a blow-up foot ball + Eve bought her long-awaited clothes-line pole.

May 28th.
      We finally left at 12.50, to go to the farm. We had a picnic lunch in the dining room with Kate & Jamie because it was overcast, then Simon & Eve + Kate + Jamie signed letters at the solicitors. After a swim, we left. The drive was beautiful.  [annotation: Windmills / [?Ystrad Aelwyd] * * [?Ystrad Aelwyd] 0650- 521-237 / [?Llanbrynmair]]
      Arrived at 8.0. at [?Cringed] [annotation: [?Llanbrynmair]] lovely wild flowers in a field of long grass, mown in patches of flat levels. Clean loos, strong river, hills all around at a distance + friendly owners. Charles (3rs 4 yrs, one month) talks incessantly. Going to brush teeth with him, we discussed the flowers, the sheep, the gravel v. grass path, that one woman who asked me the time didn't have a watch.... on and on. And he helped Simon take down the tent.

May? 10.00 we left for __Barmouth__. We bought lamb-chops, anticipating a barbeque + Thomas a pair of sandals.
      Lunch time we arrived at __Dinas__. Just as Thomas + Eve finished getting lunch ready, it started to rain, + has not really stopped for over 24 hours. After tea Simon, Thomas John, Charles + Eve went for an hour's damp ramble up on the molehill behind the campsite.
__Sunday__
      Pouring rain. Church at 11.30 at [?Duffrin] [?Llanddwyn][again]. 2 jolly women singing near us so Thomas, Eve + William got the giggles. We had lunch near a waterfall + then visited __Llanfair__ slate mine. We saw a beautiful apprentice fan @ 67 pieces of slate after he'd started down the mines at 12 years, 6 years before. Charles scared of dragons, all wearing crash-helmets.
      On we went to __Festiniog__ + wandered around the shops + had supper in a delicious, v. friendly restaurant, __Bar Harbour Restaurant__ + [?Soakingwet]. They hung our coats to dry during supper.

Monday 31st May.
		Finally it has stopped raining. Peter flew his kite. William + John climbed in the rocks. Eve got William to take a family portrait from the A frame [?stage] to the rocks.
		We set off in the van + walked in Cwm Nantcol, a superb gorge with waterfall. Simon held Charles' hand. John + Thomas were dangerous. We read about erratic boulders.
		In the afternoon we drove high up through solitary farm buildings + finally walked to + from the __Door of Ardudwy__, 3 miles altogether, now a boggy track, but once an important road from the coast into central Wales.
		Back at the campsite it is windy, Peter is flying the kite. We are about to have a barbeque.
		Simon is laughing at Charles talking all the time, even to himself. At lunch Simon drove into a bolder + smashed a bit of the numberplate.

__Menu at Sinas__

[glued-in paper slip:]
Fri l  picnic [?chicken]
    S  [?Tuna] [?Riche]    Strawb. cr.
         carrots,
Sat l  ham + rolls  Olives    apples
    S  Tongue + salad      ap + al sp
Sun l  bacon sandw.    cherries [?shortd]
    S  [?Riche] nuts [?peanutbut]  Bakewell tart
          [?cel-ery] [?mam crch]
Mon l  sausage + rolls    kiwi  [?charlse]
    S  [?artichoke hearts]
       tuna + salad       car flan    — barbeque
                                       instead
Tues l  cheese + toms    prunes
         + beans/nuts
     s  tinned ham        treacle tart

tea  Auntie Olive, rock cakes,
               [?flap] [?jacks]
]

We had a lovely Barbeque + then sat by the campfire, Simon, Thom[as] + Charles [h]ad made a fire, instead of the normal box barbeque.
Tuesday  Again it poured rain all day. We drove home via Powys Castle, which turned out to be closed, + __Montgomery__,

where Simon bought Eve a new blue handbag. We looked in the museum + saw the bread oven + the beginnings of a Norman Castle exhibition. Mention made of George Herbert.
      We stopped at the camping shop + bought Eve a blue waterproof — her old one leaked — and a large tent, a Megadome, which sleeps 4.

July 11th Sunday.
      7.10 am. off to France. We're taking only one tent, the new, 4-sleeping Megadome, + a new awning for shade. The week before we set of John fell + broke his left arm. He has a fibreglass shell on it. This is Thomas' last trip before going to Malvern.
      We went to church at __Rochester Cathedral__. Simon had telephoned ahead to find the time of the service (10.30.) + the man had been very helpful + even told him which lane to get of

the bridge for the free cathedral carpark. Simon backed into a lampost, which was fortunately already leaning backwards, + dented the bumper. There was revolting mold in the coffee pot grounds. The sermon was about forgiveness + the Prodigal Son, the Epistle on fruits of the Spirit… "patience, self-control, kindness"… as it often is at the beginning of the summer holidays.
      Amazing chevroned carvings around Romanesque arches on ground floor + first floor, + plain [illegible] arches on clerestory. Quite small.
      We left straight after receiving Communion to get to Dover.
      It was an uneventful crossing. We reached __Guincourt__ before dark. John + Charles loved the playground. We met a family from Newtownards.
Monday July 12th.
      Thomas + William walked to the bakery. We have driven towards Nancy

through farmlands. Charles keeps noticing sunflower plants because he grew one at school.

     We spent the night at Withers, Le __Long Pres.__** For supper we went to Than to the Au __Lion de Belfort.__

     It was raining, so we all slept in the van with John between Simon + Eve. Eve got clunked once in the night with his cast.
__12__th  We drove of past __Lucerne__ + over the __St. Gottard.__ Snow, old paved stone & cobbled road with pink stripe down the middle. Blinding sunshine on snow. We got out + walked around.
     About 18 miles south of the pass we came to the __St. Gottard campsite**__ It was perched on the side of a hill - we were right next to a big field. A slide led down out of our pitch into  the children's playground where William

pages.sketch_desc: Hand-drawn sketch of a mountain pass road winding through hills/mountains, with a glued-in rectangular photo (appears to show the pass road/landscape). The sketch shows curving road lines, mountain contours, and what appears to be a valley. A caption below reads 'Icy cold, earache. The St. Gottard [Pass?]' with a horizontal line separating it from the main text. The sketch appears to depict the St. Gotthard Pass approach from above.
captions: Icy cold, earache. The St. Gottard [Pass?]
pages.text:
[hand-drawn sketch of mountain pass road with glued-in photo]

Icy cold, earache. The __St. Gottard__ [Pass?]

+ Peter + John + Charles spent hours playing. There was also a little wooden swimming pool with a cover to keep in the heat at night. W, P + C swam. We shared 5 pizza among the 7 of us + had ice creams for supper.

[Glued-in illustrated map postcard of the Swiss Alps / Lake region, with the following printed labels visible on the left margin of the postcard: Mont Blanc, Grand Combin, Matterhorn, Monte Rosa, Bassodino. The map shows an aerial/relief view of the Alps with lakes and valleys. No handwritten text on this page.]

__July__ Wednesday
      Simon was unwell in the night, dizzys; fever, aching. In the morning he pottered off for a walk. There was a magnificent waterfall + very nice unobtrusive well-designed modern house. Thomas also walked.
      We packed up at lunch time + set off for __Lugano__. It's a very pretty town with a tree park + playground. We looked at large Swatches + bought icecreams in a piazza (£25) + went out in 2 paddle boats. After we bumped, Charles cried the rest of the time because he thought they were going to sink.
      We went on south + crossed into Italy. Apalling motor bikes. Lovely houses.

pages.sketch_desc: Hand-drawn sketch of a lakeside landscape viewed from the campsite. Shows a lake (Lake Piano or Lake Lugano area) with wavy shoreline on the left, a row of buildings/town in the middle, tent/camping symbols scattered on the right hillside, and what appears to be a mountainous backdrop with hatching. A castle or large walled structure is drawn on the right side. Caption written diagonally reads 'View O'Kamping Rivetta, Porlezza.'
pages.text: View O'Kamping Rivetta, Porlezza.

We turned off to Camping OK __Porlezza__ + swam in Lake Piano, between Lugano + Como.
   __Thursday__ Tried to swim but take locked at 8.15. [?Lucrhetofen] ← [?Section] [illegible]
   We set off + stopped at __Crema__ + saw the Duomo with an enormous façade, much bigger than the cathedral inside. Within, the architecture remind[s] us of English that Norman [?]. Brick, simple, dignified.
   On to __Soncino__, (the driving has been tiring, around __Lake Como__,) nowhere to stop for elevenses, a quick glance at the Villa d'Este Hotel. Simon got so cross at the ferry office that we drove instead of taking the ferry.
   At Soncino we stopped by a lovely castle + moat + the boys played football with a group of

local boys + girls. Friendlier people than in the morning.
     We spent the night at the __Cremona__ campsite, + had pizzas in a friendly restaurant where we

watched them putting pizzas in the oven. We got lost on the way out of the city centre back to the campsite.
     At every campsite John + Charles spend ages in the children's playground. At Cremona William + Peter played ping pong.
__Friday__  We set off around __Cremona__, looking at the Targetti lighting shop, the Stradivarius museum & the town hall which had a collection of lovely old violins.
     We had lunch in a self-service restaurant + couldn't get into the cathedral. Simon + the boys spent ages deciding which violin models to buy.

captions: ANDREA AMATI IL "CARLO IX DI FRANCIA" 1566
GIUSEPPE GUARNERI figlio di Andrea IL "QUARESTANI" 1689
GIUSEPPE GUARNERI detto del Gesu LO "STAUFFER" 1734

pages.text:
We left & passed through a spa town, __Salso[?Maggiore]__ Terme, & found for signs to a campsite out in the country. It is big & could be dreadful but the people are friendly & we are very fortunate to be camped right on the edge with a marvellous view. __Arizona__***** With all mod cons here, we are looking out across a scene from a mediaeval book of hours. I can see little fields from here - at least 15 of them, all of different colours & textu[res] & woodland & solitary trees, old farm houses & barns, beautiful tractored rows of hay drying in the sun. The house across the way, the children have remarked, looks like the first illustration in "James & the Giant Peach".
  The camp itself has 3 swimming pools, 2 shoots, a throbbing disco (until 3am) a laundrette & a very good restaurant where

captions: Barn across from Arizona swimming pool
pages.text:
Barn across from Arizona swimming pool

[continuing from previous page] had Sunday lunch - lasagna & roast veal + pote - for £40. I've done 2 loads of wash at vast expense £4, much to Simon's horror. Blissfully hot, but bearable with lots of trees.
	The man next to us on crutches has a very amusing green parrot called Loretto. His wife bops it when it screeds too much. She has great trouble getting

captions: House across from camping van at Arizona.
pages.text:
House across from camping van at Arizona.

it into a cage at night time.
	On Saturday evening at 6:15. an old Franciscan came round ringing a handbell "la Santa Messa alle sette". They set up an alter in the ping pong shed. The children came & went, a lovely service. I could watch the boys on the slide & swings behind the altar.
	We have spent our time eating & swimming. John & William did a [illegible] of

the playground.
		We've had a family ping pong tournament. Simon & I beat everyone, but he beat me.
	__Monday July 19th.__
		We left & went to __Parma__. What with one thing (Peter needing to pee) & another (everyone window shopping) we got to the Duomo just as it shut. We had difficulty finding a lunch place. The boys behaved dreadfully in the pink marble baptistery & cathedral & Eve got fed up & dehydrated. We then saw St Giovanni Evangelista & went back to the van. We camped at __Parma__, where there was an open air concert with a blond singer, but we couldn't go because of the children
	__July 20th.__
		*** The campsite has a resident fun fair. John & Charles & Peter went on a merry-go-round yesterday & all the boys

trampolined on a 10 bouncer.
		Today we took John & Charles again.

We had trouble finding __Torrechiara__. It is marvellous, + I frightened the boys into better behaviour. Gorgeous Romanesque arches + pleasing proportions in the open courtyard. Probably built c 1460. Frescoes in most of the rooms. Dry, sunny.
    Afterwards we had an expensive lunch down in the village in a restaurant of the hotel, which would be nice to stay in. There was only one other group of people visiting the castle - the man had great trouble convincing the ticket-seller that none of our children belonged to him. Baghoni + Benedetto were the fresco painters. The Camera d'Oro was beautiful.
    We drove on over hilly roads - beautiful scenery over Sillara pass 60% height St. Gottard. A cyclist at the top stopped to put brown paper inside his shirt, to stop his sweat

Entrance ticket to __Torrechiara__

making him cold, before whizzing to the bottom.
    At the far side we came down to __Berceto__. A lovely town with a beautiful cathedral - plain Romanesque. Tympanum outside about 1200? Crucifixion or later. Inside calm, massive, alive. John lit a candle lovely arches + crisp capitals. One of the nicest churches that I've seen. We all had ice cream. Huggledy piggledy roads
    On towards Sestri Levante. We've
